nodes.title: Stop polling immediately when aborted
nodes.why: Abort should end the loop right away so we don't keep checking after cancellation.
nodes.alternative: Ignore abort and keep polling until timeout.
nodes.tradeoff: Better control and cleanup, but requires extra listener logic.

nodes.title: Resolve as soon as check returns truthy
nodes.why: The goal is to wait only until the condition is ready, then stop.
nodes.tradeoff: Truthy check is flexible, but callers must avoid accidental truthy interim values.

nodes.title: Enforce timeout, otherwise schedule next attempt
nodes.why: Timeout prevents endless polling; if not timed out, we queue the next check after `interval`.
nodes.alternative: Use setInterval and clear it later.
nodes.tradeoff: Recursive setTimeout gives tighter control, while setInterval is shorter but easier to misuse.
